title: carduus nutans subsp. granatensis
intro: Carduus nutans subsp. granatensis, also known as the Granada-type of the Nettle-leaved Broomrape or the Granada broomrape, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carduus nutans subsp. granatensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anticancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
